name: Transform TOP
summary: The Transform TOP applies 2D transformations to a TOP image like translate, scale, rotate, and multi-repeat tiling. The background can be filled with solid color and alpha.

name: Transform XYZ CHOP
summary: The Transform XYZ CHOP is used to transform positions and vector. The difference between transforming a position vs. a vector is that a vector won't have the translation portion of the transformation

name: Transpose DAT
summary: The Transpose DAT converts rows into columns. The number of rows becomes the number of columns, and vice versa.

name: Trig POP
summary: The Trig POP is a simple operator that converts attributes that are angles into the trigonometry representation of angles (sine, cosine, tangent). Alternately, the Trig POP can convert from the sine, cosine, tangent representation back to angles using the asin, acos and atan functions. asin(x) can be thought of as "the angle whose sine value is x".

name: Trigger CHOP
summary: The Trigger CHOP starts an audio-style attack/decay/sustain/release (ADSR) envelope to all trigger pulses in the input channels. A trigger point occurs whenever the first input's channel increases acr

name: Trim CHOP
summary: The Trim CHOP shortens or lengthens the input's channels. A part of the interval can be preserved or removed. If the channels are being lengthened, the extend conditions of the channel will be used to

name: Trim SOP
summary: The Trim SOP cuts out parts of a spline surface, or uncuts previously cut pieces. When a portion of the surface is trimmed, it is not actually removed from the surface; instead, that part is made invi

name: Tristrip SOP
summary: The Tristrip SOP convert geometry into triangle strips. Triangle strips are faster to render than regular triangles or quads.

name: Tube POP
summary: The Tube POP creates rows and columns of points in a cylinder or tube shape. The points can be connected with a variety of primitives using the Connectivity menu.

name: Tube SOP
summary: The Tube SOP generates open or closed tubes, cones, or pyramids along the X, Y or Z axes. It outputs as meshes, polygons or simply a tube Primitive.

name: TUIO In DAT
summary: The TUIO In DAT receives and parses TUIO messages (received over network) into columns in the table. TUIO packets OSC bundles, so TUIO data can also be viewed in its more raw form in an OSC In DAT.	It

name: Twist POP
summary: The Twist POP performs non-linear deformations on points such as Twist, Bend, Shear, Taper, Linear Taper, and Squash & Stretch.

name: Twist SOP
summary: The Twist SOP performs non-linear deformations such as bend, linear taper, shear, squash and stretch, taper and twist. Each deformation will distort the object in one or more axes.

name: UDP In DAT
summary: The UDP In DAT is used for receiving information over a UDP connection between two remotely located computers. It captures all the messages without any queuing or buffering, and allows you to send it

name: UDP Out DAT
summary: The UDP Out DAT is used to send information over a UDP connection to/from a remotely-located computer. Use the sendBytes() or send() methods of the udpoutDAT_Class to send messages.

name: Under TOP
summary: The Under TOP places Input1 'under' Input2. The alpha of Input2 is used to determine what parts of the Input1 image are visible in the result.

name: USD COMP
summary: The USD COMP loads and imports most geometric schemas from a USD file in crate/binary or ASCII file formats with extensions as (.usd), (.usda), (.usdc), and (.usdz). Currently the USD version 0.18.9 i

name: Vertex SOP
summary: The Vertex SOP allows you to edit/create attributes on a per-vertex (rather than per-point) basis. It is similar to the Point SOP in this respect. It supports two inputs, and will inherit the first in

name: Video Device In TOP
summary: The Video Device In TOP can be used to capture video from an external camera, capture card, capture dongle, IP camera, or video decoder connected to the system. Multiple devices can simultaneously str

name: Video Device Out TOP
summary: The Video Device Out TOP routes video to output devices using their native driver libraries.

name: Video Devices DAT
summary: The Video Devices DAT provides information about all detected video devices including driver, direction (input or output), label, and name that can be referenced in the Video Device In TOP and Video D

name: Video Stream In TOP
summary: The Video Stream In TOP creates a client to receive video and audio across the network from RTSP, HLS, or SRT sources; or from a WebRTC peer via a WebRTC DAT.

name: Video Stream Out TOP
summary: Note: This TOP uses the Nvidia Hardware Encoder to create the stream and therefore requires an Nvidia GPU and Windows to operate.

name: Vioso TOP
summary: License: Only available in TouchDesigner Pro.

name: Warp CHOP
summary: The Warp CHOP time-warps the channels of the first input (the Pre-Warp Channels) using one warping channel in the second input (the Warp Curve). The Warp Curve acts as either a rate control or an inde

name: Wave CHOP
summary: The Wave CHOP makes repeating waves with a variety of shapes. It is by default 10-seconds of 1-second sine waves, a total of 600 frames. You can adjust period (frequency), phase, shape, amplitude and

name: Web Client DAT
summary: The Web Client DAT allows you to send HTTP requests to web servers from TouchDesigner. It supports GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, HEAD, OPTIONS and PATCH http methods.

name: Web Render TOP
summary: The Web Render TOP takes a URL or DAT and renders a webpage via a separate browser process that uses Chromium Embedded Frameworks, and passes the result back through shared memory. It renders non-Flas

name: Web Server DAT
summary: The Web Server DAT allows you to connect to TouchDesigner as a web client, through a web browser for example. The Web Server DAT supports HTTP, WebSockets, as well as the sending/receiving of binary d

name: WebRTC DAT
summary: A WebRTC DAT represents a peer on one end of any number of WebRTC peer-to-peer connections.

name: WebSocket DAT
summary: The WebSocket DAT receives and parses WebSocket messages. WebSockets are fast an efficient two way communication protocol used by web servers and clients. Each message is parsed and appended as a row

name: Widget COMP
summary: widgetCOMP_Class

name: Window COMP
summary: The Window Component allows you to create and maintain a separate floating or fixed window displaying the contents of any Panel or any Node Viewer.

name: Wireframe MAT
summary: The Wireframe MAT renders the edges of polygons and curves as lines.

name: Wireframe SOP
summary: The Wireframe SOP converts edges to tubes and points to spheres, creating the look of a wire frame structure in renderings. This is ideal for modelling tube structures and pipes.

name: XML DAT
summary: The XML DAT can be used to parse arbitrary XML and SGML/HTML formatted data. Once formatted, selected sections of the text can be output for further processing.

name: ZED CHOP
summary: OS: This operator is only supported under the Microsoft Windows operating system.

name: ZED SOP
summary: OS: This operator is only supported under the Microsoft Windows operating system.

name: ZED TOP
summary: OS: This operator is only supported under the Microsoft Windows operating system.

name: Alembic In POP
summary: The Alembic In POP imports geometry and point data from an Alembic (.abc) file into the POP (Point Operator) network.

name: Field COMP
summary: The Field COMP defines a spatial field sampled by the POP and physics systems to drive forces, values or deformations over space.

name: Force Radial POP
summary: The Force Radial POP applies a radial force (attraction or repulsion) to points, pushing them toward or away from a center.

name: Layer Mix TOP
summary: The Layer Mix TOP composites multiple input layers together using selectable per-layer blend modes.

name: Line Resample POP
summary: The Line Resample POP resamples polyline geometry to a new point spacing or count along each line.

name: Nvidia RTX Video TOP
summary: The Nvidia RTX Video TOP applies NVIDIA RTX Video Effects (e.g. super-resolution, denoising) to an image on supported NVIDIA GPUs.

name: Plane POP
summary: The Plane POP generates a flat grid/plane of points and primitives in the POP network.

name: Render Simple TOP
summary: The Render Simple TOP renders a 3D scene (geometry, camera, light) in one self-contained node, a simplified alternative to the Render TOP.

name: Sequencer CHOP
summary: The Sequencer CHOP arranges and triggers clips/segments along a timeline to produce sequenced channel output.

name: Serial Devices DAT
summary: The Serial Devices DAT lists the serial (COM) ports/devices available on the system in a table.

name: Text POP
summary: The Text POP generates point and primitive geometry from text strings in the POP network.

name: Tone Map TOP
summary: The Tone Map TOP maps high-dynamic-range (HDR) image values into a displayable range using tone-mapping operators.

name: Trace POP
summary: The Trace POP traces the outlines of an input image into polyline geometry in the POP network.

name: Triangulate POP
summary: The Triangulate POP triangulates input geometry (point set or polygons) into a triangle mesh.

name: Alembic Out POP
summary: The Alembic Out POP exports POP geometry and point data to an Alembic (.abc) file.

name: File Out POP
summary: The File Out POP writes POP geometry and point data out to a file on disk.

name: OAK Select POP
summary: The OAK Select POP brings point/depth data from a Luxonis OAK depth camera into the POP network.

name: ST2110 In TOP
summary: The ST2110 In TOP receives uncompressed video from a SMPTE ST 2110 network stream.

name: ST2110 Out TOP
summary: The ST2110 Out TOP sends video as an uncompressed SMPTE ST 2110 network stream.

name: TCP/IP DAT
summary: The TCP/IP DAT sends and receives messages over a TCP/IP network connection.

name: ZED POP
summary: The ZED POP brings point and depth data from a Stereolabs ZED stereo camera into the POP network.

name: ZED Select TOP
summary: The ZED Select TOP selects an image stream (color, depth, etc.) from a Stereolabs ZED stereo camera.
